task.title: Install Cloud-Native Router on the Managed and Transit Nodes
task.shortdesc: This example procedure shows how to configure the Cloud-Native Routers for management access on the overlay in a bare metal deployment.

step.cmd: Create the three virtual functions on the SR-IOV-enabled NIC on the managed node. These are ens1f0v0, ens1f0v1, and ens1f0v2 in our example. See public SR-IOV documentation for information on how to do this.

info.note: You must create the ens1f0v0 and ens1f0v1 virtual functions from the same physical function or NIC because this solution relies on the virtual Ethernet bridge on the NIC to perform switching between these two virtual functions. In our example, we use the same NIC for all three virtual functions.

step.cmd: Configure and install the Cloud-Native Router on the managed node.
step.info: Follow the steps in to configure and install the Cloud-Native Router. Configure the installation Helm chart as you normally would, but specify ens1f0v2 to be a fabric interface and ens1f0v1 to be a fabric workload interface:fabricInterface: - ens1f0v2: ddp: "off" fabricWorkloadInterface: - ens1f0v1: ddp: “off”The fabric workload interface is the OAM interface.

step.cmd: Configure and install the Cloud-Native Router on the transit node.
step.info: Follow the steps in to configure and install the Cloud-Native Router. Configure the installation Helm chart on the transit node as you normally would (with both interfaces configured as fabric interfaces).fabricInterface: - ens1f0: ddp: "off" - ens1f1: ddp: “off”
